question_text: Which of these is an example of elastic deformation?

options.label: A
options.text: Stretching a spring that returns to its original shape

options.label: B
options.text: Bending a piece of wood so it stays bent

options.label: D
options.text: Squashing a tin can permanently out of shape

correct_answer: Stretching a spring that returns to its original shape
explanation: Elastic deformation occurs when an object returns to its original shape and size after the force is removed. A spring is designed to do this. The other options show plastic deformation (permanent changes) like bending wood, tearing paper, or permanently squashing a tin can.
